title: Add Retry Logic to an API Call
description: LLM APIs fail transiently. Write a retry wrapper with exponential backoff.
problem: Write call_with_retry(fn, max_retries=3, base_delay=1) that calls fn(), retries up to max_retries times on Exception, with delay doubling each attempt (1s, 2s, 4s). Returns the result or raises the last exception.
starter_code:
import time

def call_with_retry(fn, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.01):  # small delay for testing
    # Try fn() up to max_retries times
    # Double the delay each attempt
    # Raise last exception if all retries fail
    return fn()  # fix this
hint: Use a loop with a counter. Catch Exception, sleep(delay), delay *= 2. After max_retries, re-raise.

test_cases.label: Succeeds on 3rd try
